title: Bridge Essay: Peripersonal Space and Bodily Self-Consciousness: Implications for Psychological

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Peripersonal, Space, and, Bodily, Self-Consciousness:.

**Peripersonal Space and Bodily Self-Consciousness: Implications for Psychological Trauma-Related Disorders.** — Front Neurosci. PMID: 33362457. DOI: 10.3389/fnins.2020.586605. Peripersonal space (PPS) is defined as the space surrounding the body where we can reach or be reached by external entities, including objects or other individuals. PPS is an essential component of bodily self-consciousness that allows us to perform actions in the world (e.g., grasping and manipulating objects) and protect our body while interacting with the surrounding environment.
